place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: My First Skool at 51 Fernvale Link
review_text:
Despite having dedicated teachers for each class, the teachers work together as a collective, creating a kampong spirit within the centre that we truly love. We hope our child will embrace this same spirit as she grows.

We’re especially grateful to the teachers of Patience 1, He Laoshi, Teacher Aliyah and Teacher Ann, for their care and guidance towards our little one. We can see that our child feels comfortable and safe with her teachers. She has built a close bond with them, often talking about them and even imitating them at home.

Since she started school, we have witnessed tremendous growth in her confidence, independence and ability to express herself. It has been heartening to see how much she has grown under the care of her teachers.

As parents, we are kept closely updated on our child’s development. The process feels very collaborative, with the teachers sharing thoughtful advice and answering our questions with clarity and patience. At times, they feel more like friends walking this journey with us, rather than just teachers.

A special shoutout to He Laoshi, who has been with our child for almost a year now. They share a very special bond. Even on difficult mornings, He Laoshi has a way of comforting and reassuring her, helping her settle in with warmth and ease. We are truly thankful for the love, patience and consistency she has shown our child.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: My First Skool at 51 Fernvale Link
review_text:
I am truly grateful to have such wonderful teachers at My First Skool.

They are friendly, caring, and take such good care of my daughter, allowing me to go to work without worry.

My girl is always bringing home something new that she has learned. She is learning well and has adapted wonderfully to her environment, friends, and teachers.

He Laoshi, Teacher Anne, and Teacher Aliyah are all friendly, helpful, and dedicated. I am truly appreciative of their efforts and the love and care they show to the children every day.

Thank you for making a positive difference in my daughter's growth and development. We are very happy and grateful for all that you do. ❤️

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
I lost my Oppo phone today at 930pm and called in to seletar bus depot to assist me in finding my phone. Customer officer only told me to follow the procedure to wait but I took the matter into my hands and gone down to the bus terminal at 11pm+.

Kwan voon kai, Assistant supervisor and Hiew min soon, OO, took 2 rounds to find my phone as I saw my OPPO phone GPS located at the bus terminal, in one of the bus 24.

They were in sweat and still continue to find about 1 hour for me, in every possible bus 24. I really appreciate it!!! Finally they managed to find it 12.15am in bus SG1815Y.

Thank you bus supervisors for going beyond and above to find my missing phone!!

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Conveniently located at neighborhood heartland of Lentor and Yio Chu Kang with scenic views of Seletar Simpang Kiri River leading towards Seletar Reservoir breezy greenery  away from busy cityscapes,  this bus depot occupied by SBS Transit as anchor operator and SMRT buses as tenant. It is a main bus depot with offices and maintenance facilities servicing most of the buses in Singapore. It relieved the pressure off Ang Mo Kio Bus Depot.  Noted for environmental friendly, the green roof reduces energy consumption. The bus washing system recycles 80% of the water used. Amenities include free wifi modern gym and handphone  charging stations. Bus stop with buses serving Yishun to Yishun MRT bus interchange Yishun Town Gardens Yishun Town Square Northpoint City with plenty of dinning shopping banking supermarkets library cinemas entertainments groceries sundries postage services community centre and amenities nearby. Direct bus 853 to Kallang and Geylang. Clean tidy orderly relaxing friendly fast attentive cosy comfy welcoming ambience.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
A bit secluded. Hear say there’s air con and that it is ok. Not sure if I would recommend coming here since it’s quite a distance from the nearest bus stop and facilities.

It is big, spacious and clean. The latest bus depot for a while.
Do not try to trespass! Unless you are a staff member or bus captain!

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Nice and clean working environment... Canteen here is good and cheap...

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: One of SBS Transit’s latest bus depots. Went their for a depot tour. SBS Transit has really stepped up their improvement of their bus services and maintenance of their buses and trains. Hope SBS can continue to provide wonderful service for the buses and MRT.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
A good bowl of Lormee should have that thick, gooey yet smooth gravy and packed with flavour. Unfortunately, this Lormee at Yishun Park (早碗见面 Lor mee & Satay Beehoon) , gravy was diluted and has no umami taste.

Even after adding vinegar, garlic and chilli, it’s still pretty one dimensional. The only saving grace was their fried fish, nicely fried, soft and still has that slight crunchy exterior.

Damage: $4.80 (small bowl)

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Family went there for lunch on a Saturday at around 1130H. It was not too crowded, and we managed to find a table without needing to wait. Parking was okay too.

The hawker centre was big and airy, we didn't feel hot. The tables were not so clean, and no one seemed to be going around to wipe the tables too.

We ordered (1) fish sliced soup, (2) Ming jian Kueh, (3) kids nasi lemak meal, (4) ice kachang.

Food is pretty much the standard stuff... just two things to say - (1) Shrinkflation is real - could have more fish slices in the soup, and (2) nasi lemak rice was fragrant but the chicken fillet was dry and tasteless.

Overall, still prefer Canberra hawker centre for it's range and quality of food!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
I would like to introduce a delicious Pao Fan at Stall 01-35A if you are dining at Yishun Park Market, 51 Yishun Ave 11.

Look for Chef Kelvin — he is very polite and courteous. The fried fish is freshly fried, and every grain of rice is fragrant and perfectly cooked.

Highly recommended if you are in the area!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Honestly, this is the best food I've ever tasted. You won't find this kind of food anywhere else but in Türkiye or Lebanon.I recommend everyone try this new and sophisticated food with delicious flavors.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: I stopped by for breakfast after spotting their Bubur Kampung, which looked too good to pass up. It didn't disappoint the flavors were delicious and the pricing was spot on. The place has a great atmosphere and is kept very clean. I’ll definitely be back!

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Quiet, underrated gem in the northeast. Punggol Park has a really chill vibe, especially around the big lake in the middle. Great for evening walks, light jogs, or just sitting down to unwind after a long day.

Nice mix of facilities too. There’s a Tenderbest nearby for easy bites, a gym pod if you want a quick workout, plus water coolers and vending machines around for convenience. You’ll also spot exercise stations catered for the elderly, which is a thoughtful touch. There is a nice woodball corner as well and also an open area for football.

Plenty of shaded paths and open spaces, so it never feels too crowded. You’ll see families, cyclists, and even people fishing by the pond. Simple, not overly fancy, but that’s part of the charm.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
If u are in Hougang / Sengkang area, and wanted a park to stroll or exercise.  Try this Punggol Park.
Pls note that although it called Punggol Park, it’s actually in end of upper Serangoon, Hougang area.
It’s a smaller to Bedok Reservior Park, but it’s wide enough for most outdoor exercise.
Peaceful and tranquil.  U can have a drink/ food at the eatery: Tenderbest.  They have both indoor AC and alfresco dining area.

Also, if u want to walk/ cycle more, it’s just 5mins walk away to the Serangoon Park Connector.  Which will lead u to Kallang southwards or Northwards will lead u to Punggol Point or Changi Pt via Pasir Ris PCN.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Actually, It's a Nice and quiet park with a running loop around a pond.The park with a lot of place to rest chair benches. It's shady with huge trees and bird chirping. The place is Great for walking, jogging or biking. You can come here anytime u want some peace and to de- stress through a nice and scenic walk in the park. At Night time the lights around the pond makes for a cool background. Overall a good place to bring the family and have some exercise. 🌳🌱🌿🍂🍃🍀🪺🌸💮🐦🐦‍⬛🚴🏃🚶🧎🤸

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: attended a 3 day wake at the oratory of the Church of Nativity recently. On one of the evenings, I went for a walk in the park. It was pleasant to be in nature, to reflect on the life. The park was however crowded in the evening with people exercising, cycling. walking their dogs and having picnics. Love the trees.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
It's a mind-blowing park for spending time with nature ,any time can go and spend time in peace , best place for morning time exercises and walking or running , meditation etc etc . Early in the morning the feeling here is very nice and quiet , the public toilet is also here ,
One wedding machine is also there to take your favourite drinks .

review_text:
This is a great hawker! Well-situated with a lot of spacious seating and access to open air by SIT. The food options are plentiful and they have a lot of unique and delicious food options. Overall, I was impressed with the variety and the quality and if it were closer to home, I’d come back more frequently.

Definitely recommend stopping by and enjoying! A bonus if you use FairPrice app, can save 10% on the meal!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
**Punggol Coast Hawker Centre**
84 Punggol Way, Singapore 829911

Punggol Hawker Centre offers a wide selection of mouth-watering dishes. Here, you can enjoy a variety of famous Singaporean local dishes.

On our last visit to this hawker centre, I found the delicious fried Char Kway Teow Whampoa Traditional at Punggol Hawker Centre, and I decided to order it as a takeaway.

Today, we went back for lunch and ordered the same meal again for $6 per serving.
**Rating: 7/10**

On my next visit, I plan to try other dishes from this hawker centre and recommend signature dishes from various stalls that also offer delicious options at affordable prices.

"Delicious food shared with friends."
Bon appetite!

Chris
April 17, 2026

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Punggol Coast Hawker Centre damn solid sia.

Place big and clean. No sea view lah but super near Punggol Coast MRT, walk short short only. Food choices sibei many, and got halal options like mala, chicken rice and Indian food also.

But ah: Weekday lunch and dinner confirm packed. Weekends lagi worse, be prepared to queue. Go earlier if you kiasu.

Sad sia, the Malay stall closed permanently already. Too bad :(

Still, headache only cos don’t know what to eat. Will come back again. 5 stars ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
MUST TRY!!! the roasted meat and char siew rice and it was quite cheap and delicious. The plate came with a good variety roasted pork and char siew all served over fragrant rice with sauce drizzled on top.
The meats were well-prepared overall. The roasted pork crispy and juicy nice balance of lean meat and fat, while the char siew was slightly sweet and caramelised on the edges. Portion size was decent and filling for one person. The chilli was spicy and blend well with the rice and meat. Overall, a solid and comforting meal — good for a quick and satisfying bite.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
spacious seating with many big ass fans keeping it cooling  👍🏻

unique design of the stores - they are shaped like houses!

tried the HUAY KWANG Thai Wanton Mee @ Ubi here, love it 👍🏻

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Heard about this place through a friend and decided to have a try.
It turned out to be a an evening well spent.
What i liked is the  cinematic setting. A live band, great ambience, ample photo shoot spots what else required to make it a memorable day.
We went without a booking but still got a table.
We orderd a pizza, some grilled beef and chicken skewers, fish and chips.
Staff was very attentive and served us pretty quick. We enjoyed the Food and live band made  the atmosphere was energetic. thrilled to hear some popular numbers like hotel california and the band performance was not bad at all.
Overall it was an evening well spent.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
the food was okay but not fantastic.  Pizza is quite good.. thr cauliflower gratin is a missed.. not cooked, not cheesy and cold.
pretty good live band though.
service crews are attentive and tried their best despite the big floor space.
alfresco dining

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Went on a Friday evening as a friend recommends that we visit them for the live music by “JumpStart”, singing English, Mandarin and Canto.

Order their truffle fries and chicken wing, which was good as they mixed the truffle with the fries well before serving. Service crew is attentive and friendly too.

For those driving, you may want to visit the place early as parking could be challenging.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Safra punngol has a huge ramge of facilities for families but their most famous one is the 3 story swimming pool. It is big and has slides both big and small to accomodate young children and adults to have fun using the play equipment. There is a also a bike rental and emart at is basement. Good for the residents of punngol to spend some time here for recreation

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: We had an amazing family day out at SAFRA Punggol, especially at the KidzAmaze waterpark! This place is a paradise for children with its creatively designed water play areas and slides that are both safe and exciting. The facilities are well-maintained, and the staff are incredibly friendly and attentive, ensuring that all kids have a fun and safe experience. It's the perfect spot to cool down and allow the kids to burn off some energy in a fun-filled environment. Highly recommend for families looking for a great way to spend the day!

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: One stop for some food and entertainment. Indoor water playground to bowling and coffee shop to McDonald’s it’s a good way to spend some quality time with kids. Sometimes carnival for kids in ground floor as well

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: We had a great time with my little one ( less than 1 year old ) . This place is great for 1-2 hrs of splashing entertainment. Very clean and hygienic pool and slides . The bathrooms and storage space was really well kept ( hot water - soap - quick availability) really made our time very smooth. We had a minor issue with being unable to book our tickets online ( for some reason the payment link was not visible and the rate cards online are not very accurate ) they have separate costs for members and non members ( 22$ for non members) makes sense to buy the membership card to save up. The kids love it. On Friday we had manageable crowd around. Quick bite at McDonald’s which is next door is a good option for closeby eats. Highly recommend ( carry your own water / toiletries )  for an easy experience.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Had a wonderful experience at Munchi Pancakes Sengkang! A big thank you to Eileen and Chau for their excellent service. They were both so friendly, welcoming, and attentive, making the whole experience even more enjoyable. The pancakes were freshly made, delicious, and prepared with care. It’s always nice to be served by staff who genuinely make customers feel valued. Highly recommend this outlet, and I’ll definitely be coming back again. Thank you, Eileen and Chau!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: I'm a huge fan of their pancake! Staff is friendly with a smile. Fillings - they're generous and consistently good. I even saw the staff carefully weighing out the ingredients. Plus, if you want your pancake warmed up, all you have to do is ask. It's those little touches that show they really care ❤️❤️❤️❤️.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: This stall surprised me with very delicious and rich selection of pancakes n munchi, the stall owner offers heating up the pancakes to make it even nicer. Price friendly and suggest using fair price apps for payment to enjoy additional 10% discount 👍

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Got 3 flavours, original black sesame, original hazelnut and charcoal peanut. Fillings were generous and rich, pancake was spongy and dense. Recommend eating it asap though.

Friendly and cheerful service is always the cherry on top.

Two thumbs up!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Stall owner was a very bubbly and polite person! Heard good things from my colleagues on the munchi pancake and definitely was up to standard. Will buy again!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
This mall has everything you need for a relaxing weekend. There is a kids’ play area, as well as a jogging track with a beautiful lake view.

It offers plenty of dining options, including Din Tai Fung, Song Fa Bak Kut Teh, and many others. For tea lovers, there are popular choices such as CHICHA San Chen and CHAGEE. If you’re looking for dessert, Henri Charpentier and Mister Donut are great options. Coffee lovers can also grab a drink at Starbucks.

The mall also has a supermarket, making it a convenient one-stop destination for shopping, dining, and leisure. It’s a perfect place to spend the weekend, especially if you prefer not to travel to the city centre.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Waterway Point is a large and spacious mall with a good mix of shops, products, services and plenty of food options. It covers most day-to-day needs and works well as a one-stop destination for residents in the area. That said, it gets very crowded on weekends, so be prepared for heavy foot traffic. On the plus side, there’s ample parking available, which helps. Overall, a solid and well-planned mall — just better enjoyed during off-peak hours.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Waterway point shopping mall shopping I would say clothing shops like Uniqlo n playdress.there is a popular bookstore n a toys r us.there is a miniso ...Japan home n value shop I would not really put it in a shopping category... More of home maybe ... waterway point for shopping not exactly a place .... For food I would not say that I'm so satisfied with waterway point's food options .... There r foods like fast foods like McDonalds, Jollibee,mos burger, n there r foods like kaya toast n munching snacks like old chang kee n fried banana.there is a foodcourt within the mall but NOT HAPPY WITH IT.there r foods like fish soup n yong Tau foo but there is no Indian food or Muslim food.there is a western stall in the foodcourt but I don't find much difference in eating fast foods n eating a western meal.There r also a Thai food stall which sells fried noodles n fried rice ... This foodcourt mainly Chinese foods ... For a Indian girl like me food options r very limited not only in this food court but I m talking about the whole waterway point mall ... I mean seriously the mall is so big n spacious but there is no any Indian food in it or at least a Muslim food on it ... Very disappointed ☹️ whenever I go waterway point the only options I get to eat is either fast food or Milo toast ...there r restaurants like buddy hoggies n han's ... But seriously if I want to enjoy a nice meal I have to spend so much money??? Can't I get to enjoy a nice meal for a cheaper price at a normal foodcourt???  There r take away shops like stuff'd .... Kebab, burrito, burrito bowl used to cost much cheaper than the price is costing now ... Just increased the price for kebab, burrito n burrito bowls ... N no dine in options from last time till now ... I felt not worth buying from this shop ... But I always c a long queue in this shop ... ☹️ There is a shop which similar like stuff'd...
Guzman y gormez which also sells kebab n burritos ... it has dine in options... BUT VERY EXPENSIVE... have tried there before ... Did not went back to that shop again 🤫 ... Oh n I just remembered... Waterway point has waffles.... For any1 who is a sweet tooth ... 😁 Many flavored drinks like luckin coffee ... N boost ... There r many flavored drinks shops in waterway point but not healthy... Too much sugar... I don't really encourage people to drink all this flavored drinks...
Whatever age anybody is or whether people with diabetes or not too much sugar intake n too much fast foods intake NOT HEALTHY...
Another thing which I want to tell everyone is that when eating at McDonalds please do not give your hp to any1 in the McDonald's... I have this prob with McDonalds whenever I m eating McDonalds... Since I eat at waterway McDonalds today n this prob happened again I wish to tell everyone... The person who is wearing the yellow shirt in the McDonald's I guess she is the manager's assistant she asked for my phone to do McDonalds feedback...I did not want to be rude so gave hery HP ... Customers r supposed to do the feedback not any1 else ... The McDonald's was so dirty ... I had to wipe the table before I eat ... Fly was flying around... I want to feedback but cannot bec she do feedback... The feedback she did from my phone was submitted under my name but I did do the feedback... Just bec they want to increase their outlet rating they do feedback from customers phone ... Everything they put 5 star ... Taking a customer's phone n doing feedback is very wrong ... As a customer I can make a complaint as this has happened many times ... But I just decided to just leave it ... Next time I go McDonalds when again ask for my phone I m strictly going to say as a customer I will do feedback ... I guess I have to be rude at times ... M being too nice ... Peo taking advantage... I don't want the same thing happening to any1 else so m telling every1...

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Modern large bus depot for maintenance of SBS Transit trains. Reachable by two LRT stations Tongkang and Rebjong. Plenty of fasciitis including canteen and groceries. place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: This shopping mall is highly convenient and offers an impressive variety of stores, making it a place where you can find almost anything you need. The directory includes a well-stocked supermarket, numerous fashion boutiques, electronics retailers, and a wide selection of dining options ranging from quick bites to sit-down restaurants. The layout is generally logical, though it can take some time to navigate due to the sheer size of the complex. It serves as an excellent one-stop destination for both daily necessities and specialty items. Facilities like restrooms and parking are well-maintained and accessible. Given the extensive range of shops and services available under one roof, it is a highly practical choice for weekend errands or casual browsing. However, because of its comprehensive nature, it tends to attract large crowds, especially during peak hours and weekends. Overall, it provides a highly efficient and reliable shopping experience for visitors looking to complete multiple tasks in a single trip.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: A nice, bustling shopping mall set over multiple levels. Lots of restaurants and takeaways across multiple levels, plus a large Kopitiam food court on Level 6. Besides the eateries and 'regular shops' there is a cinema, games arcades and various niche stores, plus access to the Dhoby Ghaut MRT station. Notably clean restrooms.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Plaza Singapura is very crowded but extremely useful. I had multiple skin and hair treatments here and got excellent results was 99.99.9% effective. You can trust the professionals here; they deliver exactly what they promise.😎

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: This is a long established shopping mall very popular with the locals especially when Yaohan, a Japanese brand first opened up it’s flagship super supermarket here and it was always crowded with families visiting, shopping and dining. It’s sitting immediately on the right when you face the ISTANA.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Sleek and modern mall with immense verticality, spanning up towards the 7th storey. Each floor especially the upper ones are not that large hence it's quite easy to navigate, it is very cool and air-conditioned. Aside from that, the toilets arerelativelyv clean and there is an assortment of food, entertainment, and shopping as well scattered throughout the mall. It is also conveniently linked to the large Dhoby Ghaut MRT Interchange which makes this mall very accessible.

It serves as an excellent one-stop destination for both daily necessities and specialty items. Facilities like restrooms and parking are well-maintained and accessible. Given the extensive range of shops and services available under one roof, it is a highly practical choice for weekend errands or casual browsing. However, because of its comprehensive nature, it tends to attract large crowds, especially during peak hours and weekends. Overall, it provides a highly efficient and reliable shopping experience for visitors looking to complete multiple tasks in a single trip.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: A nice, bustling shopping mall set over multiple levels. Lots of restaurants and takeaways across multiple levels, plus a large Kopitiam food court on Level 6. place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
The Seletar Mall is one of those neighbourhood malls that quietly gets the job done. It may not be the biggest or flashiest, but it is clean, well laid out, and easy to navigate. The tenant mix is practical and family friendly, with a good range of dining spots, enrichment centres, and everyday essentials. It is especially convenient for residents in the Sengkang and Fernvale area who want everything within walking distance.

The mall never feels overwhelmingly crowded, even on weekends, which makes grocery runs and casual meals much more pleasant. The cinema and supermarket are reliable anchors, and there are enough cafes to sit down and unwind. Parking is generally manageable, and access via the LRT makes it fuss free.

Overall, it is a dependable heartland mall that serves its community well. Not a destination mall, but definitely a comfortable and convenient one.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Has been ordering the food from food panda and it is the first time to eat at the actual outlet.

The dishes served are very consistent to those that delivered to my home. The delivery bag, the utensils, the food containers shows that they are customer focus.

The vermicelli ordered is really plentiful but is nice. The cabbage is a must order. The red bean soup is nice.

The services are generally good, ma y things are self services.

The only thing which is not a 5 stars is that the service crew could have exercise some flexibility. We are back there for another meal yesterday but the crew tuck us to 2 seater, which is not wrong but consider our barang barang, we requested a bigger seat but was flatly rejected mentioning this is the only seats left where there are 2 four seaters around. It is only when we are exiting out, the crew then offer a 4 seater which is a bit too late.

The service standard fall short compare to our first visit when the bosses are around.

Perhaps it is a new crew? It is our first meal of the day, we will like some comfortable space to enjoy our meals before the dinner crowds swamp in Seletar Mall.

👉 Parents’ note: Going up can be daunting, and the climbing net is best suited for older kiddos.

review_text: Lower Seletar Reservoir Park is such a peaceful and scenic spot in Singapore! 🌅 The waterfront views are beautiful, especially during sunrise and sunset, and the park is very well maintained. It’s perfect for evening strolls, jogging, cycling, or just relaxing by the water. The jetty and heritage boardwalk add a special charm, and the cool breeze from the reservoir makes it even more refreshing. A great place to unwind and enjoy nature — highly recommended! 🌿✨

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
10/10, would absolutely paddle back for this again. 🌅🚣‍♂️

The sunrise kayak + breakfast tour was such a peaceful, feel-good start to the day. Ali and Shiva were fantastic, friendly, funny, and genuinely informative. They made everything feel easy and welcoming, and were always ready to help while keeping the whole experience light and enjoyable.

It’s one of those rare activities that’s genuinely for everyone. Easy to pick up, surprisingly calming, and somehow makes you feel like you’ve got your life together before 9am. Paddle a little, enjoyed a lot, then reward yourself with breakfast by the water… what’s not to love?

Seas the day, eat your way through it, repeat. Try it!

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
The park is beautifully maintained, with a delightful variety of flowers that add color and charm to every corner. Each pathway feels inviting, filled with vibrant blooms and natural scents that make the walk feel almost therapeutic.

The gardens are especially peaceful—perfect for an evening stroll after a long day. Whether you want to unwind, enjoy nature, or simply spend some quiet time alone, this park offers the ideal atmosphere. It’s a serene escape from the noise of the city, and I’d highly recommend it to anyone looking for a calm and scenic place to relax.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
A Serene Escape, Even in a Light Drizzle

The Equatorial showers in this heartland park is a gem, especially in light rain. I recently went for a walk as a gentle shower began, and it was a surprisingly perfect experience. The rain kept the crowds away, and the sound of drops on the canopy and the reservoir's surface was incredibly peaceful.

The best part was watching the water itself. Each raindrop created a cascade of tiny, expanding ripples across the entire reservoir. It was a mesmerizing and deeply calming effect, turning the whole scene into a living, moving work of art.

It’s a wonderful reminder that you don't need to travel far for a moment of tranquility. A short, refreshing walk here, even under a light rain, is a perfect way to reset.

review_text:
Recently enrolled to MindChamps Punggol. It's a great school with good facilities, nice and engaging teachers, condusive environment for learning.

They also frequently conduct workshops to let parents know what they are learning at school and how we are able to support them at home.

Attended a Year-End Musical and Graduation concert for the first time. I was genuinely surprised by how GRAND it was! I was quite astonished to see the little Champs able to memorize such long lines, complex dance sequences, and choreography. It was easy to forget just how young they are!

The theme, "A Small Island - Little Hands, Big Dreams," was especially close to our hearts. It was truly heartwarming to watch my daughter, Evelyn, learn about Singapore and sing 'Home' so beautifully. She genuinely loves to perform, sing, and dance. I could clearly see her growth and increased confidence throughout this entire process.

A huge thank you to the entire team for teaching, organizing this incredible performance, and, of course, for taking such wonderful care of Evelyn and nurturing her growth ❤️

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
This is very great neighbourhood library with librarians who are very observant and go the extra mile. Especially I thank Ms Fazilah 9 Nov 2025 at 7.30pm. She goes the extra mile fuss free, efficient, responsive and responsible. She checked the return conveyor belt and took effort to accompany me downstairs to search shelf and all displays thoroughly. She is very detailed to get both call number and shelf number. She found the book at display within very short time and went back to her routine duty which impresses me with her work ethics.

Before Ms Fazilah helped me I tried to get the book as search shown available on shelf but cannot find. I approached another white hair lady who situated there. She gave up very fast after just finding from the usual numbered shelf which I told her I already done. Then just quickly dismissed me and gave me excuses impatiently that it may be on wrong shelf. This is although I explained I am very sure it should be somewhere in library because not many people in library and I saw no one using the book. So the previous staff did not help me much she did not even look at special display areas...

Thank you so much Sengkang library for having MS Fazilah such a rare, helpful exemplary staff who exhibits great time management and problem solving skill. I thank her for resolving patiently and quickly without excuse and not dismissing customer request.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
We recently took our little one to Sengkang Public Library, which is conveniently located right inside Compass One. It made for a really easy family outing, as we could grab a quick bite at the mall before heading up. The library itself is quite spacious and felt very clean and well-maintained, spread across two levels. Our child absolutely loved the Early Literacy Area; they have some really engaging displays and the book modules, designed like traditional fishing baskets, were a nice touch that caught our attention. It’s a bright and welcoming space, perfect for little hands to explore.

While the children's section can get a bit lively, as expected with so many excited kids, there are plenty of other quiet spots throughout the library if you need a calmer environment. My wife and I appreciated the wide selection of books for all ages, and I even spotted a dedicated 'Tween Space' which will be great for our child in a few years. There are ample seating areas, so it's easy to settle down with a stack of books and spend a good hour or two. It’s definitely a solid choice for a family afternoon out, combining learning with comfort.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
What I like in libraries: Books that are available that I originally have on my online To-Be-Read (TBR). Spacious environments. Multiple book genres.

The first thing I noticed that drew my attention when I walked in was this little Harry Potter castle, which was on top of a bookshelf. The second thing I noticed was that they had a huge selection of audiovisuals.

Once you walk a bit more towards the left, you’ll see a few bookshelves, and close to those bookshelves is a long white table. And near the white table there are 2 seats close to a window. I think that area is a really nice spot for reading, studying, and using your computers.

If you want straight, you’ll see comics, and a teen section. In between 2 bookshelves, there are 2 seats, for safe distancing. There is also a magazine section.

There is also a quick charge area. Near the quick charge area there are a few shelves of adult fiction.

Overall, I think the Sengkang Library is a nice library for spending a long duration of time with your computer, or having a book you want to read. You can also study in the area of the white table and 2 seats near the window.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: This is a two storey library and that easily makes it better. If you feel like the children section can be loud, you may find other reading spots. Conveniently located inside the mall with lots of reading books for small children. Lots of library staff too to remind the children to keep quiet while inside the library. My daughters love to hang out in this library after school.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Woodlands Waterfront Park is a relaxing and scenic park located in the northern part of Singapore. It faces the Straits of Johor, so visitors can actually see Malaysia across the water. The park covers around 11 hectares and has a 1.5-kilometre waterfront promenade, which makes it a great place for walking, jogging, or cycling while enjoying the sea breeze.
One of the most interesting features of the park is the 400-metre Woodlands Waterfront Jetty, which is the northernmost point of mainland Singapore. Walking along the jetty is a really nice experience because you get a wide view of the sea and the coastline. Many people go there to fish, take photos, or just relax while looking at the view. In the evening, the sunset from the jetty is especially beautiful and creates a calm atmosphere.
In my opinion, the park is a great place to visit if you want to take a break from the busy city. The long paths are perfect for a peaceful walk, and the cool sea breeze makes the environment very comfortable. There are also open spaces where families can have picnics, as well as a playground where children can play and climb.
Another thing I like about the park is that it is usually less crowded compared to many other parks in Singapore. Because of this, it feels quieter and more relaxing. Overall, Woodlands Waterfront Park is a wonderful place to enjoy nature, spend time with family and friends, and appreciate the beautiful waterfront scenery.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
This seaside park has an interesting history. The jetty, which is the centerpiece of the park, was a mooring location for the Malayan/Malaysian Navy when Singapore was still within the federation. It's surprising that the plot of land was still in the hand of Malaysian government till it's handed over in 1980s (almost 20 years upon the secession). The bollards at the jetty point out to the place's not-so-distant past.

Since the construction of the RTS train, it has been pretty popular place for the locals to take selfie, with the backdrop of the viaduct. Funnily enough, in spite of advisory against fishing at the jetty (posted all over), seems this is just a live and let live policy lol.

Woodlands Waterfront Park is one of my favorite spots to unwind. The park offers a beautiful, relaxing view of the Johor Strait, especially during sunset. The waterfront promenade is perfect for a peaceful stroll or a bike ride, and there’s plenty of open space for picnics with family and friends.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Woodlands Waterfront Park is a relaxing and scenic park located in the northern part of Singapore. It faces the Straits of Johor, so visitors can actually see Malaysia across the water. The park covers around 11 hectares and has a 1.5-kilometre waterfront promenade, which makes it a great place for walking, jogging, or cycling while enjoying the sea breeze.
One of the most interesting features of the park is the 400-metre Woodlands Waterfront Jetty, which is the northernmost point of mainland Singapore. Walking along the jetty is a really nice experience because you get a wide view of the sea and the coastline. Many people go there to fish, take photos, or just relax while looking at the view. In the evening, the sunset from the jetty is especially beautiful and creates a calm atmosphere.
In my opinion, the park is a great place to visit if you want to take a break from the busy city. The long paths are perfect for a peaceful walk, and the cool sea breeze makes the environment very comfortable. There are also open spaces where families can have picnics, as well as a playground where children can play and climb.
Another thing I like about the park is that it is usually less crowded compared to many other parks in Singapore. Because of this, it feels quieter and more relaxing. Overall, Woodlands Waterfront Park is a wonderful place to enjoy nature, spend time with family and friends, and appreciate the beautiful waterfront scenery.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
This seaside park has an interesting history. The jetty, which is the centerpiece of the park, was a mooring location for the Malayan/Malaysian Navy when Singapore was still within the federation. It's surprising that the plot of land was still in the hand of Malaysian government till it's handed over in 1980s (almost 20 years upon the secession). The bollards at the jetty point out to the place's not-so-distant past.

Since the construction of the RTS train, it has been pretty popular place for the locals to take selfie, with the backdrop of the viaduct. Funnily enough, in spite of advisory against fishing at the jetty (posted all over), seems this is just a live and let live policy lol.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ 5-Star Review for Wildseed @ The Summerhouse

Wah, come to Wildseed Cafe at The Summerhouse and kena first-class service sia! Staff got eagle eyes one — see us trying to 自拍 (take photo) until hand stretch like yoga pose, immediately volunteer to help take nice photos. End result so good, can use for profile picture already!

Our order taking a bit longer, but the staff never disappear like ninja. They proactively checked with the kitchen and kept us updated. Service level really power lah.

Vegetarian choices got plenty plenty, not the usual “salad only” situation. The Truffled Brie Pizza was the superstar of the day. So fragrant and cheesy that every slice disappeared faster than salary after payday. The truffle aroma attack the nose first, then the creamy brie come in for the finishing move.

Good food, beautiful surroundings, and service that makes you feel welcomed. Come hungry, leave happy, and maybe with a few new photos for social media too! 😆🍕🌿📸

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Lovely staff who were friendly and patient even on a hot afternoon. The first pizza came slightly burnt but the staff offered to redo it which we were very thankful for. Wings were quite tasty too! Not recommended to go there on a hot afternoon though as it is an open-air cafe.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
The food was quite standard, nothing particularly special or memorable. Considering the price, I personally don’t think it offers great value. The environment and dining experience were also fairly ordinary.

However, if you would like to experience the unique black-and-white house surroundings and atmosphere, it may still be worth visiting once.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Called prior to arrival to request for indoor seating due to hot weather. Given a nice table within the air-conditioned place but it was not enough to cool us down. Felt a little stuffy and our group felt warm throughout the meal.

Came on a weekday afternoon and place was almost empty.

We were presented with the lunch set menu just 2 min before promotion was over (2.30pm). We tried to order the set online at 2.35pm and was only then informed it wasn't available! We were disappointed since we thought having been presented the menu by the staff, we would be able to order the set, which is a good deal (1 drink + 1 side+ 1 main for 29.90 while usually a normal main dish is already ~32). Tried reasoning with the staff to no avail.

After we started placing our normal orders and submitting the orders, someone came and told us the manager agreed to let us order the set menu. Poor management on this part, in my opinion.

Food came nicely presented and taste legit. Mushroom soup was thick. Steak was nicely done to our request. Coffee of good size. Desserts need wait but was served hot.

Overall ambience a nice place to hang out for a couple of drinks and tea.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Beautiful restaurant, with all the warm light bulbs and live band, great atmosphere to dine in! But food was just disappointing.🥲

Soft shell crab linguine: rate 3/5 stars
- linguine not al dente.
- smallest soft shell crab i have ever eaten
- not sure what is the purpose of putting a whole chili intact on the pasta instead of chopping it. We just took it out and place it aside. The pasta wasn't even spicy at all, so i guess they expect customer to bite on that chili directly while having the pasta?
- significantly overpriced. $29 for that little bowl of pasta.

Sweet potato fries $14 (rate 4/5 stars)
- love it, but it isnt crispy at all. Its texture is rather soft n moist to me.

Sunset fizz mocktail $10 (rate 1/5 stars)
- nothing special. It tastes like the sunkist drink that u have on a plane.

Overall experience: Not worth the price. Not worth the visit.

I took a flight from Subang Airport and was thoroughly impressed. Despite being a small-scale airport, it is executed in a truly world-class manner. From drop-off to Grab pick-up, the passenger flow is thoughtfully designed and seamless.

The immigration e-gates are more than sufficient for the passenger volume, with officers on standby to assist when needed. Overall, Seletar Airport is well positioned to scale up and effectively serve underserved regional routes.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops

Can come just a half an hour before departure time. The cons are food/drink outlets limited, no shopping, and no airport lounge. As far as I know Batik, Firefly, MAS, Scoot have flights here. But they tend to be the smaller propeller planes.

I like that theres no rush, no longer lines and no echo but the only downsides are the limited choices in F&B and the longer wait times for grab.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Dishonest Behaviour at Customer Service Counter!

I visited the customer service counter today (28 April 2026) at around 3pm to top up my Frasers Points and had to wait quite a while as the customer in front of me was resolving a billing issue. After she finished, that customer kindly offered to let me have her points since she is not a Frasers member.

I showed my app to the staff member for scanning and asked her to add in those points. However, she only credited the points from my own receipt and did not include the additional points from the other customer. I specifically asked her if the points had been added, and she replied, “done already.” As I trusted her, I did not check on the spot, although I had some doubts based on her expression. That’s why I checked my account after I left the counter and realized the extra points were not added.

I understand there may be policies about transferring points between customers, and if this was not allowed, it would have been better to clearly explain that upfront. Instead, confirming it was “done” when it was not is misleading and unprofessional. I hope the staff can provide clearer and more honest communication in such situations in the future.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
The leading supermarket chain in Singapore and their premium supermarket brand.

At this outlet, you can find a more premium range of products which is not commonly found in other normal Fairprice outlets. They have a good range of fresh produces including Organic and Japanese products. Pricing is cheaper compare to another premium supermarket chain.

The place is always crowded. Suggest to come during the opening time. As all the items especially the fresh produce had just stock up.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: I'm a tourist from Taipei. On April 12th, I lost my iPhone at this store. I immediately asked the front desk for assistance. The staff told me that they checked the security camera footage and found someone had taken it, but due to privacy concerns, they couldn't investigate further and asked me to file a police report. Since the phone could not be traced, it must have been switched off. I immediately went to Sengkang to file a police report. Throughout this time, I kept contacting the police station, only to be told that the investigating officer was on leave. They finally contacted me on April 30th after I returned to Taipei, informing me that the investigation revealed a cleaning staff member had taken it and thrown it away. This was completely different from what the staff had told me. Where did things go wrong? I'm so disappointed with Singapore. This whole ordeal, with all the back-and-forth contacting the police station, has shown me that Singapore is not the efficient country I imagined.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Had a pleasant experience at One Punggol Hawker Centre. The space is one of the more modern and comfortable hawker centres in Singapore, with high ceilings, plenty of seating and a bright, airy layout that makes dining here enjoyable even during peak hours. With over 30 stalls offering a good mix of local favourites and international options, there is usually something for everyone.

The biggest strength is the overall environment. It feels clean, spacious and family friendly, and being part of the larger One Punggol community hub means you can easily combine a meal with a visit to the library or nearby facilities.

Overall, a comfortable and modern hawker centre with a good variety of food choices. Not every stall is a hit, but the pleasant dining environment and wide selection make it worth visiting if you’re in the Punggol area.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: How come this simple plate of vegetables, fish, and rice I bought from this stall costs $8.60? At a hawker center some more 😅 Got a big surprise when I was informed upon payment. I double-checked with the store owner regarding the price, and they mentioned that the fish itself is already $6!!! I hope they would advise the price for high-priced items before even plating it. Do you think it’s worth the money?? I think 菜饭店 should also indicate price tags for openness and fair pricing. The food is not fantastic, neither bad — the fish is quite dry though. 2 stars from me.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Reach there almost 8pm on a Wednesday night for dinner for end up more than half of the stores closed ( not closing or cleaning up).. Diners this timing balance about 70% of table unoccupied..  Compare to when I went to Punggol coast mall side hawker centre, fernvale hawker centre and Buangkok hawker centre even 8.10pm seat at least over 80% taken and the stores almost 90% opened or about to prepare to close during this timing.. on weekday.

Or maybe the good business store closed early as run out of serving.. balance a few stores opening.. the last store at 9pm still able to place order is Botak Cantonese Porridge..

Come again early next time to try out maybe other food choices..

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: The Thail food is horrible! Green curry become green soup! White rice was super cold rather hot, not even warm! Don’t waste your money on this stall. Tried twice! Gave up! 🤮😤😠😡🤬

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
SINGAPORE - 26 DEC 2025: 2.20pm.

One Punggol Hawker Centre is a modern hawker centre that is part of the new concept of an integrated community hub called One Punggol. Catering to Northeast Singapore residents, it has more than 30 hawker stalls, over 700 seats and automated tray return stations.

I had meals at the Indian Rojak, porridge and fried fish mee sua stalls on separate days. I find that the food quality of the Indian Rojak and Fried fish mee sua variable depending on your luck. High ceiling fans and good ventilation.

SHARING A PHOTOGRAPHY TIP:
Canon R5, 70mm, 1/2 sec, f/16, iso 200. tripod. av mode. Focus on signboard.

I used a tripod and a distance away from the entrance to get a good image in the afternoon.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Punggol Regional Library: A Visual Masterpiece in the North-East

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

As a photographer, I usually look for subjects behind the lens rather than books on a shelf, but the **Punggol Regional Library** at One Punggol is a rare exception where the architecture is as much a draw as the collection.

The Architecture & Light
From a design perspective, this isn't just a building; it’s a study in geometric lines and natural lighting. The massive glass facade allows for incredible "Golden Hour" shots if you’re positioned on the upper levels. The way the light spills across the reading tiers creates a beautiful interplay of highlights and shadows—perfect for those who enjoy **minimalist or architectural photography**.

Key Spots for Enthusiasts:
The Lens of Literature: This is the library’s "hero shot." The circular, tiered seating area provides a fantastic perspective for wide-angle frames, capturing the sheer scale of the 5-storey atrium.

The World and Us (Level 5): The curation here is top-notch, but the real prize is the outdoor terrace. You get a sweeping view of the Punggol skyline and the lush greenery surrounding the area—ideal for some urban landscape captures.
ExperienceIT & MakeIT (Level 4): For those who love macro or tech-focused shots, the 3D printers and robotics labs offer some great industrial-chic textures.

Inclusivity & Tech
Beyond the visuals, the library is a marvel of inclusive design. The **Calm Pods** and the **Borrow-n-Go** accessible walkways are thoughtfully integrated into the flow of the building. Even the way the shelves are spaced feels intentional, providing clear leading lines that make the space feel infinite.

Pro-Tips for Visitors:
Gear: Bring a wide-angle lens (16mm to 35mm equivalent) to truly capture the interior volume.
Timing: Try a weekday morning for "clean" shots without the crowds.
Post-Visit: You’re right next to the One Punggol Hawker Centre for a quick meal, and a short walk from the Waterway, which offers plenty of additional landscape opportunities along the river.

Whether you're there to research your next project or to capture the soul of modern Singaporean architecture, this place is a must-visit. 📸📚

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Overall, the library is pretty clean with many interesting facilities. What really made this a 4 star review rather than a 5 star review, is that the children's section is not to right place to read. On level 2 (children's section), i could hear kids screaming and stomping on the floor every second, so i advise to never study on level 2.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Punggol Library is an amazing space for readers and learners alike! With its fully automated system, it’s incredibly convenient to borrow and return books without the need for staff assistance. The library offers a wide range of books and digital resources, catering to various interests and age groups.

The environment is quiet, clean, and modern, making it perfect for studying, reading, or simply relaxing with a good book. The dedicated children’s section and interactive areas add a fun touch for families.

Its seamless self-service technology and thoughtfully designed spaces truly make it a library of the future. Highly recommended for anyone in Singapore looking for a productive or peaceful spot!

Punggol Library is an amazing space for readers and learners alike! With its fully automated system, it’s incredibly convenient to borrow and return books without the need for staff assistance. The library offers a wide range of books and digital resources, catering to various interests and age groups.

The environment is quiet, clean, and modern, making it perfect for studying, reading, or simply relaxing with a good book. The dedicated children’s section and interactive areas add a fun touch for families.

Its seamless self-service technology and thoughtfully designed spaces truly make it a library of the future. Highly recommended for anyone in Singapore looking for a productive or peaceful spot!

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: We had our first awful experience after several visits. Our helper was prevented from eating with us at the Terrace & Tavern because her slippers were deemed "too thin" by server Steven Yen. When I questioned why many other diners were wearing slippers, he said their soles were thicker! I said that if non-helpers wore the same, he would not dare to banish them to the pool area, away from the Tavern. What a pretentious kopi tiam.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Chomp Chomp was established in 1972 as a "hawkers centre", now better known as foodcentre. It has been upgraded a few times over the years, the latest one was end of 2025 till March 2026. Our cleaning manager gave us the tip about the great food at the very recently reopened foodhall. And she was spot on, what an absolute treat, this hidden gem. A must visit, higly recommended.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Favourite hawker centre !! I have been a regular diner at Chomp Chomp since I was young. The standard still feels around the same. I usually get the sambal stingray, oyster omelette, satay, bbq wings and carrot cake. The seats inside the centre are sometimes stuffy but the ones outside and at the back are really nice. love love love

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Lots of local dishes to choose. After renovation the place is still SMALL. Come early if you like a good seat. Better ventilation with the huge fans. Some of the stalls are no longer there.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
The XXL sugarcane juice is the hit.
Other foods are average, some of them require fairly long waiting time.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Closed from 27 October 2025 to 26 January 2026 for renovations and upgrading works. This is a great food centre with a diverse selection of hawker food. Been eating here when i was younger and living in Serangoon Gardens. My current fav char kway teow is located at this food centre and they have quite a few good stalls serving BBQ sambal stingray.

On my last visit I was surprised to see that Chomp Chomp isn't as crowded as it used to be back in the day. On the bright side, it’s way easier to get a seat now.

review_text: Well built! The station is linked to the mall, too! I’ve took the newest train to Harbourfront, and it was AMAZING! The trains were silent! review_text: Just want to share so next time you won't be confused. place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Punggol MRT Station is clean, spacious, and really well-organised. The interchange setup with the LRT makes transfers easy, and the signs are super clear so you won’t get lost even during busy hours. Trains come frequently, and the station staff are usually helpful when you need directions. The surrounding area is convenient too — Waterway Point is directly connected, so grabbing food or doing errands after your commute is very easy. Overall, a reliable and comfortable station to travel from.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
• Free entry & hours: Compass One is a public shopping mall, so entry is free. The mall’s main contact page lists opening hours as 10:00am–10:00pm daily, while individual stores may open earlier or close later depending on the tenant.

• What & why go: Compass One is a major Sengkang town-centre mall with 180+ brands and a wide mix of shopping, dining and everyday services. It is described by the mall as one of the area’s most extensive and fully integrated malls.

• Top features to see: You’ll find a mix of fashion, food, beauty, electronics and family-friendly outlets, plus anchors like Cold Storage, McDonald’s, Starbucks, POPULAR and the Sengkang Public Library inside the mall.

• Getting there: The mall is at 1 Sengkang Square, Singapore 545078. The official “Getting Here” page says to take the North East Line and alight at Sengkang Station (NE16); buses also serve the mall.

• Parking & practical access: Compass One has on-site parking with published hours for cars and motorcycles. The parking page lists operating hours as 7:00am–11:00pm daily.

• Best for quick visits & family stops: With groceries, food, library access and many daily-need stores, it works well for a short stop, errands, or a family meal after Sengkang Station.

---

⚠️ What to keep in mind

• Store hours vary: Not every shop follows the mall’s main opening hours; some open much earlier for breakfast or close sooner on holidays. Check the tenant page if you are going for a specific store.
• Holiday changes: The mall publishes festive operating hours and some stores may close or shift timings during major holidays.
• Parking charges apply: Parking is paid and has no grace period, so short visits can still incur a charge.
• Facilities are useful but simple: The mall offers customer service on Level 3 and free Wi-Fi with membership perks, but it is still mainly a neighbourhood mall rather than a luxury destination.

review_text:
Compass 1 shopping mall linked directly to mrt station n Lrt station... Lots to shop like miniso, clothing shops,Japan home,value shop ... Lots to eat ... Bun shops,cakes,old change kee,Mr bean also at compass 1 ... There is a ananas food shop at just outside the entrance to compass 1 which has dine in options... Also a Muslim shop right beside it which is a takeaway place ... Boost,fruit juice shop,pizza,stuff'd and lots more food at compass 1 ... Weekdays r usually less crowded compared to weekends...

review_text: **Compass One** is a well-planned neighbourhood mall directly linked to Sengkang MRT and bus interchange. Designed by **DCA Architects**, the mall features a clean, contemporary layout with good natural light and clear circulation. Practical mix of shops, dining, and community spaces makes it convenient and comfortable for daily use.
